records.ety: [F. caution a security, L. cautio, fr. cavere (For scavere) to be on one's guard, to take care (orig.) to be on the watch, see; akin to E. show.]

defs: A careful attention to the probable effects of an act, in order that failure or harm may be avoided; prudence in regard to danger; provident care; wariness.

quotes: The Parliament would yet give his majesty sufficient caution that the war should be prosecuted.

defs: Precept or warning against evil of any kind; exhortation to wariness; advice; injunction.
quotes: In way of caution I must tell you.

defs: A pledge, bond, or other security for the performance of an obligation either in or out of judicial proceedings; the promise or contract of one not for himself but another; security.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cautioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Cautioning.]

defs: To give notice of danger to; to warn; to exhort [one] to take heed.
quotes: You cautioned me against their charms.
